user_query:
I’m trying to keep our family weekends smooth and happy using a tiny agenda script my daughter wrote, but it’s a bit messy and gives the wrong output. Please review and refactor the script so it produces a clean, deterministic weekend agenda and is easier to maintain. I care about a simple, reliable result so we can plan dinners and time together without fuss.

What I’m giving you (all paths are relative to the workspace):
- Input data: input/events.csv
- Current script: scripts/family_planner.py
- Current config: config/settings.yaml
- Expected agenda for verification: expected/expected_agenda.csv

What I need you to do:
1) Code review and refactor:
   - Standardize configuration keys used by the script to exactly these names: time_format, cancel_tag, include_cancelled, output_columns.
   - Update the code to read those keys from config/settings.yaml and not hardcode them.
   - Correct logic so that, when run with the provided input and config, the script:
     • Filters out events whose title contains the cancel_tag if include_cancelled is false.
     • Removes exact duplicate events (same date, start_time, end_time, title).
     • Sorts events by date (ascending) and then start_time (ascending).
     • Computes duration_minutes correctly as the number of minutes between start_time and end_time.
     • Writes columns in the order specified by output_columns from the config.
   - Refactor into small, readable functions with clear docstrings (keep behavior simple and local—no external dependencies).

2) Modify configuration:
   - Update config/settings.yaml to use the standardized keys and values so the data in input/events.csv is parsed correctly and the cancelled event is excluded. Concretely, set:
     • time_format: "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"
     • cancel_tag: "[cancelled]"
     • include_cancelled: false
     • output_columns: [date, start_time, end_time, title, duration_minutes]

3) Run and verify:
   - Run the script so it reads input/events.csv and writes output/agenda.csv using the config.
   - Acceptance criterion: output/agenda.csv must be byte-for-byte identical to expected/expected_agenda.csv.
   - Suggested command (you can keep this interface or improve it as long as it remains executable locally):
     python scripts/family_planner.py --config config/settings.yaml --in input/events.csv --out output/agenda.csv

4) Write a short code-review report:
   - Create docs/REPORT.md summarizing:
     • Key issues you found (e.g., misleading config keys, incorrect duration math, lack of sorting/deduping).
     • The refactoring decisions and why they help family-friendly maintainability.
     • How to run the script and how you verified the output against expected/expected_agenda.csv.

5) Draft a family message:
   - Create comms/family_message.txt with a warm, friendly note that focuses on our family time (I don’t care about career talk—just that we’re happy together!).
   - After a short intro, include a section titled "Our Plan" with one line per agenda entry using this exact format so it can be cross-checked:
     YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM-HH:MM Title
   - Use the entries from output/agenda.csv so the six lines match the finalized agenda exactly.

Deliverables to create/update:
- Modified: scripts/family_planner.py and config/settings.yaml
- Generated: output/agenda.csv (must match expected/expected_agenda.csv exactly)
- New docs: docs/REPORT.md
- New message: comms/family_message.txt

Please keep everything self-contained and runnable locally. Thank you for helping me keep our weekends happy and organized! All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
